description: Feature-parity matrix: Opentrons robot-server HTTP API <-> SiLA2 connector. The connector re-exposes the full opentrons robot-server HTTP route shape; when durable labware plans are configured, raw HTTP gripper/extension actuation is rejected so it cannot bypass the labware ledger. This matrix records which HTTP functions ALSO have a first-class SiLA2 equivalent. sila_support: 'supported' = SiLA equivalent exists and is exercised against the simulator; 'unclear' = a SiLA path exists but full parity needs hardware confirmation; 'unsupported' = intentionally HTTP-only (no SiLA equivalent by design).

entries.notes: Liveness + robot model over HTTP; SiLA exposes IsSimulating and server identity. Full field parity (api_version, fw) is HTTP-only.
Attached modules register dedicated SiLA features at startup; HTTP /modules lists them.
SiLA move is per-mount deck coordinates; HTTP /robot/move semantics differ. Confirm parity on hardware.
E-stop/door surfaced via MachineStatus; exact HTTP path + payload parity to confirm on hardware. Drives Pitfall #2 post-move error-state check.
Primitive plunger moves exist only on SiLA; there is no direct low-level HTTP aspirate/dispense route (HTTP uses the protocol engine).

entries.sila_element: Mix / TouchTip / ProbeLiquidLevel / tracked liquid moves / Transfer / verified liquid classes
entries.notes: Atomic advanced liquid workflows share the OT3API hardware lock and are covered through the real simulator and SiLA gRPC codec.

Sensor-verified tip lifecycle commands atomically move to the supplied deck position and act under one hardware lock. MotionControlFeature.EmergencyStop is the single pre-emptive, robot-wide halt path. HTTP performs tip handling through the protocol engine.
Flex gripper primitives exposed on SiLA; HTTP exposes gripper only via the protocol engine.

Executes locally allowlisted plans with a durable location-to-labware identity ledger, official Flex gripper waypoints, module-state and pickup-width checks, fail-closed restart/cancellation recovery, and raw-gripper bypass prevention.
Automatic probe-based routines on SiLA; HTTP calibration endpoints differ in shape. Confirm on hardware with a probe attached.
SiLA GetPosition returns per-mount deck coordinates; HTTP /robot/positions returns preset named positions (deprecated).
Server/robot configuration is intentionally HTTP-only; per AGENTS.md, transport/server config is not modelled as a SiLA feature.
Blink-to-identify is HTTP-only; the equivalent operator action on SiLA is SetLights.
The connector exposes motion/liquid primitives, not the Opentrons protocol/run engine. Full protocol orchestration stays on HTTP (or the UniteLabs workflow layer).
Protocol upload/management is HTTP-only by design.
